currentCM.name: Admiral D. K. Joshi
currentCM.description: Current Lieutenant Governor of Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

exCMs.details: Andaman and Nicobar Islands is a Union Territory of India, not a state.Therefore: It does not have a Chief Minister. It is governed by the Lieutenant Governor appointed by the President of India.Administration is controlled directly by the Central Government of India.
exCMs.disclaimer: The list of ex-Chief Ministers is not applicable for Andaman and Nicobar Islands due to its status as a Union Territory.
exCMs.importantNote: Please refer to the current Lieutenant Governor for administrative information about Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
exCMs.Why No Chief Minister?: As a Union Territory, Andaman and Nicobar Islands does not have a Chief Minister. The administrative head is the Lieutenant Governor, who is appointed by the President of India and oversees the governance of the territory on behalf of the central government.
exCMs.Current Lieutenant Governor: Admiral D. K. Joshi is the current Lieutenant Governor of Andaman and Nicobar Islands, responsible for the administration of the territory.
exCMs.Unlike states, Union Territories are adm: Delhi & Puducherry have elected governments and Chief Ministers.But Andaman and Nicobar Islands does not have a legislative assembly, so there is no CM post.
exCMs.type: Andaman and Nicobar Islands is a Union Territory of India located in the Bay of Bengal. Its capital is Port Blair. Unlike Indian states, it does not have a Chief Minister or Legislative Assembly and is directly governed by the Central Government through a Lieutenant Governor. The islands are famous for their natural beauty, coral reefs, beaches, tribal communities, and the historic Cellular Jail, which played an important role during India’s freedom struggle.

exCMs.details: Chandigarh is a Union Territory of India, not a state.Therefore: It does not have a Chief Minister. It is governed by an Administrator appointed by the President of India.Administration is controlled directly by the Central Government of India.
exCMs.disclaimer: The list of ex-Chief Ministers is not applicable for Chandigarh due to its status as a Union Territory.
exCMs.importantNote: Please refer to the current Administrator for administrative information about Chandigarh.
exCMs.Why No Chief Minister?: As a Union Territory, Chandigarh does not have a Chief Minister. The administrative head is the Administrator, who is appointed by the President of India and oversees the governance of the territory on behalf of the central government.
exCMs.Current Administrator: Banwarilal Purohit is the current Administrator of Chandigarh, responsible for the administration of the territory.
exCMs.Unlike states, Union Territories are adm: Delhi & Puducherry have elected governments and Chief Ministers.But Chandigarh does not have a legislative assembly, so there is no CM post.
exCMs.type: Chandigarh is a Union Territory of India located in Northern India. It serves as the capital for both Punjab and Haryana states. Unlike Indian states, it does not have a Chief Minister or Legislative Assembly and is directly governed by the Central Government through an Administrator. Chandigarh is famous for its modern architecture, urban planning, Rock Garden, Sukhna Lake, Rose Garden, and Capitol Complex.

exCMs.Current Administrator: Praful Khoda Patel is the current Administrator of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu, responsible for the administration of the territory.
exCMs.Unlike states, Union Territories are adm: Delhi & Puducherry have elected governments and Chief Ministers.But Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu does not have a legislative assembly, so there is no CM post.
exCMs.type: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu is a Union Territory of India located in Western India. It was formed by merging the former Union Territories of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu. The capital is Daman. Unlike Indian states, it does not have a Chief Minister or Legislative Assembly and is directly governed by the Central Government through an Administrator. The territory is famous for its beaches, Portuguese heritage, Dudhani Lake, and the town of Silvassa.

exCMs.details: Jammu and Kashmir is a Union Territory of India, not a state.Therefore: It does not have a Chief Minister. It is governed by a Lieutenant Governor appointed by the President of India.Administration is controlled directly by the Central Government of India.
exCMs.disclaimer: The list of ex-Chief Ministers is not applicable for Jammu and Kashmir due to its status as a Union Territory.
exCMs.importantNote: Please refer to the current Lieutenant Governor for administrative information about Jammu and Kashmir.
exCMs.Why No Chief Minister?: As a Union Territory, Jammu and Kashmir does not have a Chief Minister. The administrative head is the Lieutenant Governor, who is appointed by the President of India and oversees the governance of the territory on behalf of the central government.
exCMs.Current Lieutenant Governor: Manoj Sinha is the current Lieutenant Governor of Jammu and Kashmir, responsible for the administration of the territory.
exCMs.Unlike states, Union Territories are adm: Delhi & Puducherry have elected governments and Chief Ministers.But Jammu and Kashmir does not have a legislative assembly, so there is no CM post.
exCMs.type: Jammu and Kashmir is a Union Territory of India located in Northern India. It has two capitals: Srinagar (summer) and Jammu (winter). Unlike Indian states, it does not have a Chief Minister or Legislative Assembly and is directly governed by the Central Government through a Lieutenant Governor. The region is famous for its natural beauty, including Dal Lake, Gulmarg, Pahalgam, and Mughal Gardens.

exCMs.details: Ladakh is a Union Territory of India, not a state.Therefore: It does not have a Chief Minister. It is governed by a Lieutenant Governor appointed by the President of India.Administration is controlled directly by the Central Government of India.

exCMs.Current Lieutenant Governor: Radha Krishna Mathur is the current Lieutenant Governor of Ladakh, responsible for the administration of the territory.
exCMs.Unlike states, Union Territories are adm: Delhi & Puducherry have elected governments and Chief Ministers.But Ladakh does not have a legislative assembly, so there is no CM post.
exCMs.type: Ladakh is a Union Territory of India located in Northern India. Its capital is Leh. Unlike Indian states, it does not have a Chief Minister or Legislative Assembly and is directly governed by the Central Government through a Lieutenant Governor. Ladakh is famous for its stunning landscapes, including Pangong Lake, Nubra Valley, Khardung La Pass, and its rich cultural heritage with numerous monasteries.

exCMs.details: Lakshadweep is a Union Territory of India, not a state.Therefore: It does not have a Chief Minister. It is governed by a Lieutenant Governor appointed by the President of India.Administration is controlled directly by the Central Government of India.

exCMs.Why No Chief Minister?: As a Union Territory, Lakshadweep does not have a Chief Minister. The administrative head is the Lieutenant Governor, who is appointed by the President of India and oversees the governance of the territory on behalf of the central government.
exCMs.Current Lieutenant Governor: Mani Shankar Aiyar is the current Lieutenant Governor of Lakshadweep, responsible for the administration of the territory.
exCMs.Unlike states, Union Territories are adm: Delhi & Puducherry have elected governments and Chief Ministers.But Lakshadweep does not have a legislative assembly, so there is no CM post.
exCMs.type: Lakshadweep is a Union Territory of India located in Southwest India. Its capital is Kavaratti. Unlike Indian states, it does not have a Chief Minister or Legislative Assembly and is directly governed by the Central Government through a Lieutenant Governor. Lakshadweep is famous for its pristine beaches, snorkeling, and diving opportunities in the Arabian Sea.
